name: Mage AI Data Pipeline
what_it_does: Open-source data pipeline tool that combines an IDE-like interface with production pipeline capabilities. Mage uses a block-based pipeline model (load → transform → export) with live code execution for fast iteration. REST API and Python SDK for pipeline management and triggering. Supports batch and streaming pipelines, dbt integration, and ML model training workflows. Positioned as a modern alternative to Airflow.
use_cases: Build and trigger data pipelines via Mage's REST API for agent-driven ETL automation
Orchestrate ML training pipelines with Mage's block-based model that chains data loading, training, and evaluation steps
Run dbt transformations as part of agent data preparation pipelines using Mage's dbt block integration
Build streaming pipelines that process Kafka events for real-time agent data preparation
Automate data quality pipelines using Mage's built-in block-level assertions and testing framework
not_for: Enterprise-scale orchestration at Airflow parity — Mage is newer with a smaller plugin ecosystem
Complex dependency management across hundreds of pipelines — Airflow or Prefect handle large-scale DAG orchestration better
Real-time sub-second streaming — Mage supports streaming but Flink or Kafka Streams are better for low-latency
best_when: You want a modern, developer-friendly alternative to Airflow with a UI-driven block-based pipeline model and simpler deployment.
avoid_when: You need the extensive plugin ecosystem and production battle-testing of Airflow at enterprise scale — Airflow's maturity may outweigh Mage's better DX.

auth.notes: API key for service-to-service access. OAuth for user authentication. Self-hosted: auth optional in development mode. Mage Cloud adds proper auth. Keys passed in X-API-KEY header.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Self-hosted is free (Apache 2.0). Mage Cloud has a free tier.
pricing.paid_tiers: Mage Cloud Pro and Enterprise for managed hosting with additional features
pricing.notes: Mage core is Apache 2.0. Self-hosting is free. Mage Cloud for managed deployment. Competitive with Prefect and Kestra pricing for managed tiers.

performance.rate_limits: No Mage-imposed limits for self-hosted; Mage Cloud limits based on plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns JSON with run status and error messages. Pipeline run failures include block-level error context. Webhook payloads contain pipeline run metadata. Error messages are developer-friendly.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Pipeline runs create new execution instances — not idempotent by default. Trigger deduplication available in some configurations. Agent-side idempotency required for retry safety.
known_agent_gotchas: Self-hosted Mage requires managing application state — use persistent volumes for production deployments
Block execution order follows Mage's DAG topology — agents cannot guarantee execution order without proper block dependencies
API authentication is optional in dev mode — production deployments MUST enable auth explicitly
Mage's streaming pipelines run as continuous processes — lifecycle management differs from batch pipelines
dbt integration requires dbt project to be pre-configured — agents cannot dynamically create dbt projects via API
Pipeline run status API must be polled — no built-in wait mechanism for run completion
Mage's block-based architecture differs from Airflow tasks — mental model shift required for teams migrating from Airflow

security_score_components.security_notes: Apache 2.0 open-source. Auth optional in dev mode — must enable for production. SOC2 for Mage Cloud. Self-hosted security is operator-managed. Secrets management via environment variables or Mage's secrets store.
